description: GitHubData.pr_by_repo_branch uses dict[tuple[str, str], PrInfo] which cannot round-trip through Pydantic JSON serialization. model_dump_json() joins tuple keys as comma-separated strings (e.g. 'org/repo,main'), and model_validate_json() fails to parse them back into tuples. While this field is currently only used in-memory and never serialized, it violates the FrozenModel contract that models should be serializable, and will break if anyone adds JSON persistence or logging that calls model_dump_json().

_lookup_pr fallback for agents without a known repo (remote/modal agents) returns the first PR matching by branch name when iterating pr_by_repo_branch. If multiple repos have PRs on the same branch name, the result depends on dict insertion order. This is unlikely with the mng/ branch naming convention but is a latent source of nondeterminism.

The variable 'repo_path' is used in two separate loops in fetch_github_data: first as the result of _get_github_repo_path (line 113), then as the iteration variable from cwd_by_repo.items() (line 125). Although not a bug (the loops are separate), it reduces readability.
